release_review.summary: Ask Flower linked context is injected through the published Floret v0.3.89 SupplementalContext API without changing the Runtime Service compatibility window.
checked_surfaces: Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1; Ask Flower linked-context projection is internal to Flower's AI runtime and the published Floret SupplementalContext API rather than a runtime-service protocol version change
Floret is consumed only from the published github.com/floegence/floret v0.3.89 module with GOWORK=off validation and no replace, go.work, package-manager link, or local sibling path wiring

Ask Flower context actions remain Redeven product records that are validated, persisted with the user turn, and projected into Floret current-turn supplemental context rather than Runtime Service request or response fields

File browser, file preview, and editor-preview context remains path/metadata-only; terminal long selections are length/truncation metadata only; attachments project as attachment_metadata without upload URLs or file bodies

flower.context_action.injected records only aggregate injection metadata and a context hash; raw linked context, file contents, terminal bodies, and upload URLs do not enter Runtime Service diagnostics
Desktop attach/start, Local UI health, startup reports, sys.ping, Env App maintenance UI, SSH runtime bootstrap, gateway lifecycle, and Runtime Service control methods are unchanged
release_review.same_window_rationale: The compatibility window is unchanged because the feature is implemented inside Flower's AI runtime integration with the published Floret runtime module and does not add or change Runtime Service protocol messages. The Runtime Service snapshot protocol remains redeven-runtime-v1, Desktop-managed startup still uses structured machine presentation and startup reports, sys.ping and attach semantics are unchanged, Env App maintenance UI and gateway lifecycle are unchanged, and SSH/runtime bootstrap compatibility does not require a newer Desktop or Runtime binary pair.
